legacyFolders.migrationGuidance: API DTO compatibility remains at delivery boundaries; migrate individual DTO families only when application commands/results already exist.
Keep legacy services stable while extracting cohesive workflows into app/application, app/domain, app/ports, and adapters.
Move transformer behavior behind explicit mapping helpers or adapters when touched by source-data or event-contract slices.
Preserve producer compatibility while new publication paths use event publisher ports and adapters.

representativeAdoptions.noRuntimeSplitRationale: This representative adoption improves package-level design modularity inside the existing ingestion deployable. It does not introduce a new deployable service, database, queue, or operational ownership boundary.

legacyFolders.migrationGuidance: Cost policy runs behind the target CostProcessingPort. Move surviving packages to target-owned domain names, then delete the legacy root after release/Kubernetes/canonical-QA parity.
Cashflow policy runs behind the target CashflowProcessingPort. Move surviving packages to target-owned domain names, then delete the legacy root after release/Kubernetes/canonical-QA parity.

Position reduction and replay run behind the target PositionProcessingPort. Move surviving packages to target-owned domain names, then delete the legacy root after release/Kubernetes/canonical-QA parity.

representativeAdoptions.noRuntimeSplitRationale: App-local Compose and Compose-backed CI now use one target deployable with two consumers and one atomic financial unit of work. Cost, cashflow, and position remain in-process modules because they share transaction ordering, scale, trust, and commit boundaries; valuation remains separate. Registry/Kubernetes rollout and legacy package removal remain governed follow-up work.
